comment_content:
The slides are quite dense but short. (1-3 slides one concept)
If you have a math background the first month of the lectures are probably easy that you can just read in 1 sitting
After that start to learn dfferent topic in crypto.
Class Overview:
• Lecture 01: Functions
• Lecture 02: Modular Arithmetic
• Lecture 03: Introduction to Cryptography
• Lecture 04: One-Key Block Ciphers
• Lecture 05: Linear and Nonlinear Functions
• Lecture 06: A Design Paradigm of One-Key Block Ciphers
• Lecture 07: Several One-Key Block Ciphers
• Lecture 08: Advanced Encryption Standard
• Lecture 09: Modes of Operation for Block Ciphers
• Lecture 10: Introduction to Public-Key Cryptography
• Lecture 11: The RSA Public-Key Cipher
• Lecture 12: The ElGamal Public-Key Cipher
• Lecture 13: Two Mutual Authentication Protocols
• Lecture 14: Two Key Establishment Protocols
• Lecture 15: The Kerberos Authentication Protocol
• Lecture 16: A Digital Signature Scheme
• Lecture 17: Hash Functions
• Lecture 18: The Digital Signature Standard
• Lecture 19: Public-Key Infrastructures
• Lecture 20: Keyed Hash Functions and the HMAC
• Lecture 21: Random Number and Key Generation
• Lecture 22: Secret Sharing Schemes
• Lecture 23: Anonymity on the Internet
• Lecture 24: A Review of Major Cryptographic Primitives

comment_content:
Content is pretty easy and lays good foundations

It covers everything exactly as shown on the profs website, in order at:
https://www.cse.ust.hk/faculty/cding/COMP3631/

For those with weak math background, I recommend extra study on Modular arithmetic, and to understand the math basis of RSA and El-Gamal well from the slides and YouTube etc.

ONLY AROUND 20-25 SLIDES PER LECTURE :0 so it is very easy to catch up. Since there is so few slides, they can be dense at times with a lot of notation that you should wrap your head around as early as possible so you can follow during class optimally.

I say its "outdated" because your grade is 56% homework... which everyone AI's and gets nearly full marks. I assume this course has been running long before AI made differentiating students on homework obsolete. IMO it should be changed if they want to force students to struggle with problems.

(If its like this next year you would be a fool to not check your answers with AI on the homeworks)

No midterm, 4 homeworks, open book final with laptop and wifi off.
comment_teaching:
The prof is a well established veteran in the field if you look at his accolades its quite impressive.
He covers the slides slowly and clearly. Not too engaging though, but hes open to questions throughout and after class.

When people had midterms in other subjects he moved the homework deadlines later which is kind.

You can follow up the concepts yourself to see cool applications and security weaknesses, there are plenty of resources online.
comment_workload: Low/medium due to there being NO midterm and only 4 homework tasks. Just make sure you don't miss any.
comment_grading:
Grading is "absolute" so no curve allegedly. But they still cant give everyone an A+, but really the lowest you will get if you try is a B range.   

56% 4 homeworks, 4% Attendance, 40% Final

Since everyone gets near perfect on the homework, the final is all you really have to snatch up that 25% A range.

The final is OPEN BOOK AND LAPTOP NO WIFI OR AI. So just organise it well and you will be fine.

COMPLAINT: They did not enforce the no wifi or AI rule at all!!!  there where no checks and in the 3 hour exam they barely looked at your screen. I suspect people cheated which is unfair and selfish  when we are competing for As.

I ended up with a B+ with 100/100 in all 4 homeworks and 71/100 Final. Which I'm very happy with since my final wasn't the best. Not sure about attendance but I think I got all 4%

comment_content:
Discrete Mathematics (linear/non-linear functions/modular arithmetic)
Design criteria of a cipher/protocol
ideas following One-key block cipher
Public key cryptography
hash functions
i am kinda lazy to copy the whole thing and format them so it is what it is.
One thing to note is that the content is not too technical as mentioned by prof. Ding. You don't have to dive deep into the math or step-by-step process on AES or DES etc.
Still, the content is quite useful/insightful.
comment_teaching:
The slides are not made for self-study as said by prof. Ding. However, he have little to add on the ideas on the slides most of the time. With a few times where actual insightful information were provided. So the slides themselves are in a position where it is not quite designed for self-study NOR lecture-taught.
I personally think that this justify the completely-open-book exam, such that you have to do research on your own to better understand the topics.
comment_workload: 4 assignment over the course of the entire semester. each costs 3-4 hours max. Also no midterm. Light workload definitely.
comment_grading:
Lecture attendance 4%
4 assignments 56% (14% per assignment)
Final Exam 40%
i agree that the assignments are quite important with this grading scheme. Can't lose out too much on the assignment as they have a higher weighting than the final exam.

The final exam is completely open book. You can bring your laptop,tablet whatever to the final exam. The only thing is that you should turn off its wifi and don't try to host a local AI on your machine. Anything downloaded before (the lecture slides/your cheatsheet etc) can be used in the exam.

I got a B+ from getting 73 in the final exam with full attendance but 97% assignments only. I personally think i scored pretty low because it was completely open book and Ding's marking are quite generous. So i think you can definitely do better than what i did.

comment_content: 像是离散数学里的密码学，主要内容有离散数学基础、加密性质及其实现方法、加密算法介绍等等。课件比较清晰有条理。纯粹的数学课，难度点主要加在数学上，内容蛮有意思
comment_teaching: 教授上课有点催眠，但课件自学也能看懂。教授人是很好的，课后问问题也是亲切有耐心
comment_workload: 一共只有四个作业，基本是平均分散在学期之中，而且给的时间很长（2-3weeks做一个），难度不算太高，能很好地辅助理解，设计不错。唯一不好就是HW占56%grade，不怎么能失误（mean一般90+），平时压力大些
comment_grading: 没有Midterm，HW56% attendance4% Final40%。Final能带电脑或平板但不能开wifi，改卷结束直接把成绩单贴canvas真是活久见，排名11/50，拿A，grading还是不错的
